description: An underground planning engineer designs the optimal shape for a stope (the underground excavation for ore removal) to maximize ore recovery while remaining stable.

nodes.task_description: Import the geological block model for the area to be mined.

expected_outcome.value: The block model showing ore grades is loaded.

nodes.task_description: Use a stope shape optimizer tool to automatically generate a financially optimal stope design based on the block model grades and a cut-off grade.

expected_outcome.value: An initial, financially optimal stope shape is generated.

nodes.task_description: Manually adjust the stope shape to ensure it is practical to mine and geotechnically stable, then finalize the design.

expected_outcome.value: A final, practical, and stable stope design is created and ready for inclusion in the mine schedule.
